title: Glomera adenandroides
taxonomy: Glomera adenandroides is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the genus Glomera, which is a group of terrestrial orchids that are native to Australia and New Guinea. The family to which Glomera adenandroides belongs is Diosmaceae, but Glomera is also sometimes classified under the family Orchidaceae. Further, it is placed in the tribe Coelogyneae, which is a group of orchids that are characterized by their terrestrial growth habit and showy flowers. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and phylogenetic studies.
benefits: Glomera adenandroides, also known as the Spider Orchid, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Asia. Although limited research has been conducted on the health benefits of Glomera adenandroides, it is believed to have potential medicinal properties. The plant contains various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities.
